content.problemStatement:
In a Circular City, there are nnn houses, numbered from 1 to n and arranged in 1,2,...,n,1,2,...
Chef needs to deliver packages to mmm (m<=n) houses.

Chef is initially at house 1. Chef decides an integer xxx and stops after every xxx houses. i.e- if n=7n=7n=7 and x=2x=2x=2. He will stop at 1,3,5,7,2,... He may deliver a package when he stops at a house. His work is done when all the packages are delivered.

What is the minimum number of times Chef has to stop, if he can choose any xxx ?

**Note**: Starting point (1) is also counted in number of stops

###Input:


First line will contain n,mn, mn,m, denoting number of houses and number of packages respectively.
Next line contains mmm distinct space separated integers denoting the houses
###Output:
Single line containing an integer denoting minimum number of stops.

###Constraints


3≤n≤10003 \leq n \leq 10003≤n≤1000
1≤m≤n1 \leq m \leq n1≤m≤n

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For first input,
If Chef chooses x=3x=3x=3, he will stop at 1, 4, 2 before delivering all the packages.

Explanation:
For second,
If Chef chooses x=1x=1x=1, he will stop at 1, 2, 3, 4 before delivering all the packages.
